heading: Create a Database Trigger
body:
- Authenticate a MongoDB Atlas user:

  Use your [API Access Lists endpoint](atlas-admin-api-access) to
  log in to the App Services CLI:

  ```shell
  appservices login --api-key="<API KEY>" --private-api-key="<PRIVATE KEY>"
  ```

- Pull your App's latest configuration files:

  Run the following command to get a local copy of your configuration files:

  ```shell
  appservices pull --remote=<App ID>
  ```

  By default, the command pulls files into the current working directory. You can
  specify a directory path with the optional `--local` flag.

- Add a database Trigger [Trigger configuration file](atlas/app-services/reference/config/triggers) to the `triggers` subdirectory in your
  local app files.

- Deploy your changes:

  Run the following command to [deploy](atlas/app-services/apps/update) your changes:

  ```shell
  appservices push
  ```

> **Note:** Atlas does not enforce specific filenames for Trigger
> configuration files. However, once imported, Atlas will rename
> each configuration file to match the name of the Trigger it defines.
